user_query:
I’m setting up a “DMX listening mode” on my machine so I can flip into a focused vibe when I’m bumping old-school tracks. Using my local preferences and library metadata, please generate cross-shell enable/disable scripts, a structured profile, and a short status report — all grounded in the provided inputs. Do not require admin rights and don’t call out to the Internet.

Inputs (read and extract content from all of these):
- input/preferences.yaml
- input/library.csv
- input/dmx_quotes.txt

Produce all of the following outputs under the workspace:
1) output/enable_listening_mode.sh
   - POSIX shell script that, when sourced, sets per-session environment variables defined in preferences.yaml (e.g., HIPHOP_MODE, EQ_PRESET, PROMPT_PREFIX) and creates a shell alias with the specified name and message effect from preferences.yaml. The end result should let me run the alias name in a POSIX shell and see the configured message printed.
   - Make it safe to source multiple times (avoid duplicate alias definitions or errors).
2) output/disable_listening_mode.sh
   - POSIX shell script that unsets the same environment variables and removes the alias if present (no errors if already removed). Also safe to source multiple times.
3) output/Enable-ListeningMode.ps1
   - PowerShell script that, when dot-sourced, sets the same environment variables for the session and defines an equivalent command with the same name and message effect (a function is fine in PowerShell if alias semantics differ). Must not require admin rights and must be safe to dot-source multiple times.
4) output/Disable-ListeningMode.ps1
   - PowerShell script that clears the same session environment variables and removes the command definition if present (idempotent behavior).
5) output/session_profile.json
   - A structured JSON file with the following fields exactly:
     {
       "mode_name": string,                    // from preferences.yaml
       "env_vars": object,                    // key-value pairs from preferences.yaml
       "alias": { "name": string, "message": string },
       "banner": string,                      // use the first non-empty line from input/dmx_quotes.txt
       "library_stats": {
         "total_tracks": integer,             // count all rows in input/library.csv excluding header
         "dmx_tracks": integer,               // rows where artist == "DMX"
         "dmx_total_duration_seconds": integer // sum of duration_seconds for DMX rows
       }
     }
   - Values must be computed from the inputs (do not hardcode).
6) output/STATUS.md
   - A brief human-readable status summary that includes:
     - The mode name and the banner line used.
     - Counts from library_stats (total tracks, DMX tracks, and total DMX duration in seconds), derived from input/library.csv.
     - A short note on what the enable/disable scripts do and how to activate them (e.g., “source output/enable_listening_mode.sh” for POSIX and “. .\output\Enable-ListeningMode.ps1” for PowerShell).

Additional constraints:
- Only use the provided inputs to drive values in scripts and outputs. No network use and no extra inputs.
- The alias/function in both shell variants must reflect the alias name and message drawn from preferences.yaml.
- For the banner, take the first non-empty line in input/dmx_quotes.txt exactly as written.
- Ensure all required outputs are created at the specified paths.

I grew up on DMX, so please keep the naming intact from the inputs (don’t sanitize the content beyond what’s needed for correct shell quoting). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
